thesis: Silver is in a structural supply deficit with industrial demand (solar, EVs, AI) growing faster than mine supply. The gold/silver ratio near 80:1 is historically elevated, suggesting significant upside as the ratio mean-reverts. Investment demand provides a floor during uncertainty.

drivers: Supply deficit widens as solar demand accelerates
Investment demand surges on recession/geopolitical fears
LBMA/COMEX inventories draw down to critical levels

risks: Silver thrifting in solar cells reduces intensity faster than expected

base_case.assumptions: Steady industrial demand growth of 3–4% annually; 1–2 Fed rate cuts; moderate investment flows; gold/silver ratio normalizes to 65:1.

catalysts.event: FOMC meeting — watch for rate-cut guidance

key_risks: Silver substitution in electronics and solar
